title: Longest Path in a DAG (Java / Python)
general_notes: No double deduction (except in point 5). Do not deduct again for an error already penalised in an earlier point. For example: if the virtual source node is missing, base the recurrence marks on whether the student applies the longest-path DFS correctly on their own graph.
This rubric applies to both Java and Python submissions. The Java method is calc(int N, int[][] A); the Python function is calc(N, A). Both are correct.

The algorithm uses memoized DFS on a DAG. A virtual source node 0 is added with edges to all nodes 1 to N, so that a single DFS from node 0 finds the globally longest path. The final result is dfs(0) - 1 to account for the virtual edge.
Alternative correct approaches (e.g., topological sort + DP, DFS from every node with memoization) are also acceptable if they produce the correct result in O(N + M).

rubric_points.name: Graph construction and virtual source
rubric_points.target: An adjacency list is built from A, and a virtual source node 0 is added with edges to all nodes 1 to N so that a single DFS covers all nodes.

buckets.label: Wrong
buckets.description: No adjacency list is built from A; the algorithm operates directly on the edge array or returns 0 without constructing a graph.

buckets.label: Semi
buckets.description: An adjacency list is built from A correctly, but the virtual source node 0 (connecting to all nodes 1 to N) is missing, so the algorithm can only find paths starting from a specific node rather than the globally longest path.

buckets.label: Correct
buckets.description: Adjacency list is built from A, and node 0 is added with directed edges to every node from 1 to N, enabling a single DFS from node 0 to consider all nodes as potential starting points.

rubric_points.name: Memoization: initialisation and cache check
rubric_points.target: A memo array is initialised to -1 for all nodes, and if memo[node] != -1 the cached value is returned immediately without recomputation.

buckets.label: Wrong
buckets.description: No memo array is used; the DFS recomputes all subproblems on every call, leading to exponential runtime on large DAGs.

buckets.label: Semi
buckets.description: A memo array is present but initialised to 0 instead of -1, making it impossible to distinguish an uncached node from one whose longest path is 0; or the cache check is present but uses the wrong sentinel value.

buckets.label: Correct
buckets.description: memo is initialised to -1 for all nodes; at the start of each DFS call, if memo[node] != -1 the cached result is returned immediately, ensuring each node is computed at most once.

rubric_points.name: DFS base case: leaf node
rubric_points.target: When a node has no outgoing edges (leaf), the function returns 0.

buckets.label: Wrong
buckets.description: No base case is present; the function crashes or loops indefinitely on nodes with no outgoing edges.

buckets.label: Semi
buckets.description: A base case is present but returns the wrong value (e.g., returns 1 instead of 0 for leaf nodes), causing an off-by-one error in all computed path lengths.

buckets.label: Correct
buckets.description: When graph[node] is empty (no outgoing edges), the function returns 0, correctly representing a path of length 0 from a leaf.

rubric_points.name: Recurrence: longest path over children
rubric_points.target: For each child of node, sol = max(sol, dfs(child) + 1) is computed; the result is stored in memo[node] before returning.

buckets.label: Wrong
buckets.description: Children are not iterated, or no max is taken; the function returns a constant or only considers one child.

buckets.label: Semi
buckets.description: Max over children is attempted but dfs(child) is not incremented by +1 (edge not counted), or the result is not stored in memo[node] before returning (breaking memoization for this node's callers).

buckets.label: Correct
buckets.description: sol = max(sol, dfs(child) + 1) is applied for every child; memo[node] is set to sol before returning, so subsequent calls to dfs(node) use the cached result.

rubric_points.name: Overall correctness
rubric_points.target: calc returns the correct length of the longest directed path in the DAG for all valid inputs.

buckets.label: Wrong
buckets.description: Returns 0 or an incorrect length for most inputs.

buckets.label: Semi
buckets.description: Returns correct results for simple linear graphs but fails for branching DAGs, or the -1 adjustment for the virtual source is missing (returning a result one too large), or the algorithm times out due to missing memoization.

buckets.label: Correct
buckets.description: Returns the correct longest path length for all valid DAGs in O(N + M) time; the virtual source adjustment (dfs(0) - 1) is applied correctly.
